text:
31 AUGUST 1959

I. THE COMMUNIST BLOC
USSR pledges no nuclear tests as long as Western powers do not resume tests.
Algerian rebel wounded to be treated
token_text: 31 august 1959 i the communist bloc ussr pledges no nuclear tests as long as western powers do not resume tests algerian rebel wounded to be treated

text:
the USSR.
Typhoon strikes Taiwan Strait area.
No new incidents reported between Chinese Communist and Indian border forces.

II. ASIA-AFRICA
Laotian Ministry of Defense claims some success in northern Laos.
Thailand--Dissatisfaction with Premier Sarit increasing among ruling military group.
Indonesia--Dissidents threaten to sabotage oil installations in southern Sumatra.
Japan--Visits to Communist China arranged by anti-Kishi conservatives.

III. THE WEST
De Gaulle tells French cabinet he is looking for Algerian solution which will satisfy "reasonable" Frenchmen; De Gaulle said still to fear the army

text:
II. ASIA-AFRICA

Situation in Laos

The Laotian Defense Ministry on 29 August claimed that the army had, with some success, begun a series of attacks designed to reduce the area of rebel control in northern Laos.

A high-ranking military official told that the situation in Sam Neua Province "was all right for the moment," but that he considered General Amkha, the over-all commander in northern Laos, ineffective and doubted there would be any real improvement until he is relieved. He also said that during a recent inspection tour he had found the morale of officers and noncommissioned officers high, but because of negligence on the part of their officers, the morale of the troops was poor.

Communist partisans in areas of northeastern Luang Prabang Province reportedly have executed progovernment village leaders and are forcibly indoctrinating the population. Government forces are reported mounting security sweeps in that area, but Communist tactics apparently make rebuilding popular confidence in the government a formidable task.

a report evaluated as possibly true, Vietnamese Communist cadres in Ubon, in northeastern Thailand, met frequently during July to discuss plans for resisting Thai repatriation efforts and for lending support to insurgents in Laos by secretly sending food, weapons and, in some instances, men. During the Indochina war, the Vietnamese refugee communities in northeastern Thailand provided significant support to the Viet Minh and they are in a position to supply similar support for Laotian Communist partisans.

The airlift between Hanoi and Dien Bien Phu which temporarily ceased on 19 August apparently resumed on 27 August with the

text:
scheduling of two North Vietnamese LI-2 transports over this route. On 29 August these two planes were scheduled to fly from Hanoi to Dien Bien Phu with at least one shuttle flight between Dien Bien Phu and Na San before returning to Hanoi. There were no load descriptions but these aircraft might be carrying gasoline and oil to Na San and Dien Bien Phu.

The Indian External Affairs Ministry around 23 August proposed to the Laotian ambassador that India and Laos exchange military missions, but stressed that any such Indian group would function only as a "good-will mission" and would not be concerned with the present hostilities. New Delhi's proposal is probably designed to bolster Indian influence

text:
the area and is indicative of New Delhi's recent more sympathetic attitude toward Vientiane.

Both Hanoi and Peiping on 29 August commented on American aid to Laos. A North Vietnamese Foreign Ministry statement strongly protested the American action, calling it a violation of the Geneva Agreement. The statement concluded with a call for "emergency measures" to reactivate the International Control Commission in Laos. A Peiping press commentary accuses the United States of seeking a "pretext for stepped up interference in Laos." The commentary denounced UN or SEATO intervention as part of an American plot to extend the Laotian "civil war." Both statements asserted that the United States must bear full responsibility for the consequences of its activities
